titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the D. Michael Cheers Papers and Photographs
titlestmt.author: by Jessica Tai

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 23, 2020 at 03:02

did.unittitle: D. Michael Cheers papers and photographs

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection contains photographic prints, slides and negatives by photojournalist D. Michael Cheers. The bulk of the photographs contain images used for articles in Ebony South Africa , of which Cheers was managing editor from 1995-2000. Other material includes Cheers' photojournalism work, including his coverage of Jesse Jackson's presidential campaigns, the Rainbow Coalition, Jackson's meetings with Nelson Mandela, and photographs of Hugh Masekela.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from the William Reese Co. on the James Weldon Johnson Memorial Collection of Negro Arts & Letters Fund, 2011.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The D. Michael Cheers Papers and Photographs is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: D. Michael Cheers Papers and Photographs. James Weldon Johnson Collection in the Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 32 (CDs): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 32 (3.5 inch disks): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 33 (audiocassettes): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

bioghist.head: D. Michael Cheers
bioghist.p: D. Michael Cheers is a photojournalist, writer, documentary filmmaker and educator. He received a B.A. and M.S. in Journalism, and an M.A. in African Studies from Boston University. He received his Ph.D. in African Studies and Research from Howard University. Cheers was a co-editor of Songs of My People, a book and touring exhibition that featured the work of fifty African American photographers. From 1995 to 2000, Cheers was the managing editor of Ebony South Africa magazine, which was developed for the citizens of post-apartheid South Africa. Since 2006, Cheers has been an Associate Professor of Photojournalism in the School of Journalism and Mass Communications at San Jose State University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

Information included in the Content Description note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
Folder titles enclosed in quotation marks indicate transcribed writing from original enclosures.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six series: I. Ebony South Africa photographs and records, circa 1980s-2003. II. Jesse Jackson photographs, 1983, 2003. III. Boston Globe photographs, circa 1975-1976. IV. Personal and professional papers, 1988-2004. V. Electronic media, 1998. VI. Audiovisual material, 1999.

did.unittitle: Ebony South Africa photographs and records

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The South African edition of Ebony magazine was published between 1995-2000. D. Michael Cheers was the managing editor of the magazine for the duration of its publication. Material in this series includes negatives, transparencies, photographic prints, articles, and other material related to Ebony South Africa . Subjects documented include South African writers, artists, musicians, and politicians, including Nelson Mandela and Hugh Masekela. The series also includes the magazine's business records such as correspondence, memos, story ideas, and article drafts.
Photographic film in this series include negatives as well as transparencies.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is primarily arranged by project or magazine article. Business records are arranged at the end of the series.

did.unittitle: "Chicco Twala and Mike Mangena" (negatives and prints)
"A Tribute to Mahlathini Nkabinde" (article and prints)
"The Police Brutality Debate" (articles, negatives, and prints)
"Shilowa The Man and His Vision" (article and negatives)
"Are These the Love Affair of the Century" (article and negatives)
"10 Ways to Turn Your Partner On" (article)
"7th All Africa Games" (articles, negatives, and prints)
"How to Make Your Money Work" (negatives and prints)
"Lessons I Learnt From Mommy" (negatives and prints)
"A Salute to Sports Heroes" (article and prints)
"Makkie Setshedi: Mmabatho's Top Black Businesswoman" (articles, negatives, and prints)
Terry McMillan article material (clippings, negatives, and prints)
Nelson Mandela Presidential campaign photographs (negatives and prints) and Ebony publication
Nelson Mandela and Jesse Jackson photographs
"Date With a Dish" (article, negatives, and prints)
"Celebrities and their Unusual Hobbies" (article, negatives, and prints)
"Date With a Dish" (article, negatives, and prints)
"A Salute to the Diversity of Today's Black Women" (article and negatives)
"Thabo Letsebe: SA's Basketball Sensation" (article and negatives)
"How I Made A Million" (article and prints)
"100 Years of African Beauty, Hairstyles and Fashion" (article and prints)
"25 Most Fascinating Black South African Women of the 20th Century" (article and prints)
"Health Update: Create Your Own Diet" (article and print)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes mounted and loose black and white and color prints, ranging from 11" x 14" to 13" x 19."

did.unittitle: Ebony South Africa cover design material
Ebony South Africa business records: correspondence, story ideas, clippings, and article drafts
Ebony South Africa business records: correspondence, memos, story ideas, and clippings
Ebony South Africa business records: article drafts, and memos
Ebony South Africa business records: correspondence, memos, story ideas, and clippings
Ebony South Africa business records: story ideas, clippings, correspondence, and memos
"Ebony South Africa: Black by Popular Demand" poster

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes photographic prints and negatives of the Jesse Jackson presidential campaigns of 1984 and 1988, and the activities of the National Rainbow Coalition, which Cheers was assigned to cover while working as a staff reporter for Johnson publications.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material in this series is arranged by project.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes mounted black and white and color photographic prints

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes loose and mounted black and white and color photographic prints

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material in this series is arranged in its received order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes photographic negatives that Cheers shot while working as a photographer for the Boston Globe from 1975-1976. Images include coverage of campus events, sports games, festivals, and other Boston area events.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material in this series includes slides and papers relating to Cheers' work as a photojournalist, and as Editor for Ebony South Africa . Also included is correspondence and a book jacket for Songs of My People.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material in this series is arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Content included in the slides may overlap with the Jesse Jackson and Ebony South Africa series.

did.unittitle: Songs of My People book jacket
Personal papers: photographs, business card, and workshop flier
Still Grazing by Hugh Maskela and Michael Cheers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of an audiocassette, microcassette and Hi8 videocassette.
